description: Salvadoran criminal organization specializing in drug trafficking, founded in the 1990s by José Adán Salazar Umaña, Juan Umaña Samayoa, and Roberto Herrera. It transported drugs from Colombia through northern El Salvador to North America, bribing law enforcement and officials. The cartel was dismantled by Salvadoran authorities in 2018.

country_links.evidence_quote: The cartel transported drugs manufactured by Colombian drug cartels through northern El Salvador towards North America.
country_links.context: The Texis Cartel transported drugs manufactured by Colombian drug cartels.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Texis Cartel trafficked drugs from Honduras, through northern El Salvador, and to Guatemala.
country_links.context: The Texis Cartel trafficked drugs to Guatemala and stole cars there.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Texis Cartel trafficked drugs from Honduras, through northern El Salvador, and to Guatemala.
country_links.context: The Texis Cartel trafficked drugs from Honduras.

country_links.evidence_quote: It typically worked with drug cartels from Colombia and Mexico.
country_links.context: The Texis Cartel worked with drug cartels from Mexico.
